purpose: Claim ledger for the Fable 5 pipeline mission (docs/_handoff/2026-06-11-fable5-mission-prompt.md). Doctrine: optimistic intake (ASSUMED) -> verification (VERIFIED / PARTIAL / REFUTED) -> deepen (gsr_application). Nothing ASSUMED or REFUTED may enter the build plan. This file is the research loop's heartbeat; commit it every cycle.

status_legend.ASSUMED: heard from a source, not yet checked
status_legend.VERIFIED: confirmed against official docs/changelog/live test (evidence cited)
status_legend.PARTIAL: works with caveats (caveat stated in verifier_evidence)
status_legend.REFUTED: checked and false/stale; never build on it

claims.claim: This environment runs Claude Code 2.1.173 with skills: deep-research, loop, verify, run, code-review, security-review, simplify, update-config, session-start-hook, fewer-permission-prompts, claude-api; /goal is NOT exposed in the web harness skill registry.
claims.verifier_evidence: Phase E live test, see 2026-06-11-mission-run-notes.md Addendum: the /loop skill's scheduling backend (CronCreate/ScheduleWakeup) is not exposed here either; 30-min cadence emulated with a persistent Monitor tick. /lanes and /resume-lane DID surface as invocable skills mid-session (run-notes Phase E table corrected).
claims.gsr_application: Goal conditions are emulated as AND-checklists + independent verifier agent; /loop drives checkpoint cadence

claims.claim: YouTube blocks transcript/player API requests from cloud-provider IPs; rung 1 (youtube-transcript-api) fails IpBlocked from this container even though youtube.com returns 200.
claims.source: mission doc + live test video kOKavHnlPik
claims.verifier_evidence: Live test 2026-06-11: youtube_transcript_api raised IpBlocked
claims.gsr_application: Seed-corpus transcript mining must run on Daniel's Mac (rung 2 kit pre-staged in 2026-06-11-transcript-pull-kit/)

claims.claim: Invidious instances can substitute for direct YouTube caption access from a cloud IP (rung 4).
claims.verifier_evidence: Live test 2026-06-11: inv.nadeko.net lists caption tracks but serves 0-byte caption bodies; /videos endpoint disabled; yewtu.be 403; pipedapi.kavin.rocks timeout. Metadata sometimes available, transcript content not. Matches 2026 reports that YouTube's PO-token requirement broke caption proxying.
claims.gsr_application: Do not plan rung 4 as a backbone; opportunistic only, as the mission doc already says

claims.claim: /goal needs Claude Code >= v2.1.139; evaluator checks the condition every turn; /goal show + /goal clear exist.
claims.source: mission doc Phase E table (cited code.claude.com/docs/en/goal, verified 2026-06-11 by the doc's author)
claims.verifier_evidence: Doc-verified 2026-06-11 per mission contract; env version 2.1.173 satisfies the floor; feature simply not surfaced in the web harness (CL-001)
claims.gsr_application: Daniel's Mac CLI sessions CAN use /goal for build lanes; web sessions emulate

claims.claim: Sample-transcript style hype ('auto mode never does anything crazy', 'the model is smarter than you, lean on it for all thinking') is reliable engineering guidance.
claims.source: video corpus sample transcript (mission doc R1)
claims.verifier_evidence: Contradicts canon s15 prompt-hygiene ruling and Anthropic's own confirmation-gate guidance; the mission doc itself flags it as hype. Logged per doctrine so it is on the record as not-build-on-able.
claims.gsr_application: None. Confirmation gates stay.

claims.claim: Rundown Creator returns errors as HTTP 200 with a JSON error body, so every client must read the body.
claims.verifier_evidence: Long-established in repo across /api/rc-* implementations; re-confirmed in CLAUDE.md current state
claims.gsr_application: Any new RC automation (Lane 4 sync) wraps calls in a body-checking client

claims.claim: The live DB already has production_lower_thirds / lower_thirds_variations (PR #50's migration applied) while main's deployed code still reads 'graphics' — the rename ships code-side only when PR #47 + #50 + #52 merge.
claims.source: PR #50 body ('Migration listed in Supabase list_migrations (verified applied)') + open-PR audit 2026-06-11
claims.verifier_evidence: Read-only Supabase SQL 2026-06-11: production_lower_thirds, lower_thirds_variations, production_graphics all EXIST (0 rows each); 'graphics'/'graphics_variations' are GONE; episodes=48, guests=175.
claims.gsr_application: First build slice (answer 1A) must sequence on this: DB is ahead of deployed code

claims.claim: cleanupPeriodDays is honored when set in a project's .claude/settings.json (not only user settings).
claims.source: own assumption while wiring Phase E
claims.verifier_evidence: Claude Code settings schema (update-config skill, 2026-06-11): cleanupPeriodDays is a general settings.json key; settings load user -> project -> local and merge. Caveat: THIS session's permission layer blocks settings.json edits (self-modification guard), so applying it falls to Daniel or a Mac session (one-liner recorded in run-notes).
claims.gsr_application: Mission Control needs Daniel's Mac session JSONL to survive; if user-only, the plan tells his Mac to set it once

claims.claim: Production is in a schema-skew window: the live DB no longer has the 'graphics' table (renamed by PR #50's applied migration) while the deployed main-branch app still queries 'graphics', so the deployed lower-thirds pages/API hit a missing table until PR #47+#50+#52 merge and deploy.
claims.source: deduction from live-DB check + open-PR audit
claims.verifier_evidence: Live DB table list 2026-06-11 (graphics absent) + main's code references graphics (PR #50 diff renames ~20 call sites, unmerged). Impact bounded: all lower-thirds tables have 0 rows, so no data loss, but the deployed pages error against a missing relation.
claims.gsr_application: Raises urgency of Daniel's Q16 merge decision: merging the PR stack closes the skew window. Build plan sequences slice 1 after that merge.

claims.claim: claude-devtools: desktop app over ~/.claude transcripts (cross-session search, token attribution, subagent trees); zero outbound network per SECURITY.md.
claims.verifier_evidence: v0.5.0 2026-05-13, MIT, 3.5k stars; brew install --cask claude-devtools
claims.gsr_application: Mission Control forensics layer: ADOPT (one-tap yes, 8B)

claims.claim: claude-code-log: HTML session index + TUI resume; v1.4.0 Jun 3 2026.
claims.verifier_evidence: uvx claude-code-log@latest --open-browser; MIT
claims.gsr_application: WATCH: overlaps claude-devtools

claims.claim: claude-self-reflect: local cross-session semantic memory (single Rust binary, SQLite+HNSW, no keys core); tool renamed csr_reflect_on_past in v9.
claims.gsr_application: WATCH: memory not live oversight; later lane candidate

claims.claim: ccusage per-session usage works BUT the live monitor (blocks --live) was REMOVED in v18; burn rate only via blocks/blocks --active.
claims.gsr_application: ADOPT as weekly cost lens, not a live monitor

claims.claim: claude-squad only tracks sessions it launches; experimental autoyes auto-accept mode.
claims.verifier_evidence: v1.0.18, AGPL-3.0, 7.8k stars
claims.gsr_application: SKIP: autoyes against the David Rule's spirit; Agent View covers it

claims.claim: Official Agent View (claude agents): all BACKGROUND sessions on one screen, Haiku row summaries, peek/reply, scriptable --json. Gaps: interactive terminals invisible until backgrounded (/bg or left-arrow); NO token columns; no history search.
claims.verifier_evidence: Research preview since v2.1.139 (~2026-05-13); disableAgentView kill switch exists
claims.gsr_application: Mission Control live layer: ADOPT + the /bg habit; claude agents --json feeds a lanes.json sessions block (the extend-lanes build)

claims.claim: claude-view (tombelieber): live dashboard over ~/.claude JSONL seeing ALL sessions incl. interactive; telemetry ON by default (CLAUDE_VIEW_TELEMETRY=0).
claims.gsr_application: WATCH: fallback if the /bg habit annoys; disable telemetry if ever adopted

claims.claim: mission-control (builderz-labs): full orchestration platform auto-discovering ~/.claude sessions; 5.3k stars in 4 months.
claims.verifier_evidence: MIT, self-hosted Next.js+SQLite, updated 2026-06-11
claims.gsr_application: SKIP: replaces the workflow (anti-churn) instead of extending lanes

claims.claim: Supabase Realtime current recommendation: broadcast-from-database (realtime.broadcast_changes trigger + private channel + setAuth) in a client component via @supabase/ssr; postgres_changes still works but 'does not scale as well'.
claims.verifier_evidence: Official docs label Broadcast the recommended method; needs RLS policy on realtime.messages + trigger setup
claims.gsr_application: Live status on /workflow, /lower-thirds, /episodes without refresh; build realtime features broadcast-first

claims.claim: Supabase Cron (pg_cron module) + Supabase Queues (pgmq, GA) are the official scheduled-job + pull-queue pair; pgmq_public exposable via Data API so the Mac worker polls read() with visibility timeout (durability + retries for free).
claims.verifier_evidence: Queues is pull-only (Mac still polls); pgmq_public exposure must be explicitly enabled + RLS-considered
claims.gsr_application: Supersedes the hand-rolled jobs-table plan in canon s12 orchestration (refinement, same shape); distribution backbone

claims.claim: Vercel deploy hooks: per-project unauthenticated URLs (URL = the secret), auto-cancel duplicates, 5/project Pro; observe via REST API with account token (Bearer + ?teamId).
claims.verifier_evidence: Treat hook URL as a password; personal tokens team-scoped, set expiration
claims.gsr_application: Automation can redeploy the dashboard and watch the deploy land before reporting success

claims.claim: Basecamp 4 API: OAuth tokens expire after 2 WEEKS (refresh via launchpad); per-project webhooks exist (18+ recording types, 10 retries backoff); rate ~50 req/10s/IP; flat routes canonical; no chat events.
claims.verifier_evidence: Webhook cap per account (507 when exceeded)
claims.gsr_application: Basecamp lane MUST build the token-refresh loop before any webhook consumer ships; push-style sync viable

claims.claim: ProPresenter official HTTP API (openapi.propresenter.com): trigger presentations/cues/props/looks/macros/timers/messages/clear-layer; chunked-HTTP status streaming; TCP wrapper. NO server-side dry-run mode. Version era is now ProPresenter 17+ subscription (not 7.x); Companion module v1.1.1 2026-06-04 active.
claims.verifier_evidence: Safety is procedural: read-only GET verification, then triggers on TEST machine only; pin + record test-machine version before trigger work
claims.gsr_application: Defines the authorized ProPresenter lane: verify-by-GET, fire-on-test, live rig human-yes per canon s15

claims.claim: YouTube Data API quota CHANGED: videos.insert costs 1 unit in a dedicated Video Uploads bucket, default 100 uploads/day (old era ~6/day equivalent). publishAt still requires privacyStatus=private. Unverified API projects: uploads locked private until compliance audit passes.
claims.verifier_evidence: Third-party sources date the change Dec 2025; the practical GSR gate is the ONE-TIME project audit
claims.gsr_application: Distribution unblocked quota-wise; publishAt fits the Monday-4PM webstream model; START THE AUDIT EARLY

claims.claim: n8n vs Claude Code 2026: complementary not substitutional; for a solo non-developer on Claude Code + cloud APIs, self-hosted n8n adds ops surface without capability; Cron/Queues + gated routes serve the guardrail role.
claims.verifier_evidence: Vendor-side evidence; revisit only if many always-on event integrations outgrow Cron/Queues/webhooks
claims.gsr_application: ADR-0012's n8n deferral HOLDS; seed-corpus n8n videos are technique sources only

claims.claim: /goal real since v2.1.139; evaluator = prompt-based Stop hook on small fast model every turn; /goal clear exists BUT '/goal show' does NOT (bare /goal prints status). 4000-char conditions; works in -p; survives --resume.
claims.verifier_evidence: Mission doc's '/goal show' is wrong - correction recorded in run-notes
claims.gsr_application: Mac lanes: /goal <AND-list> with a turn-cap clause as cost brake

claims.claim: /loop: s/m/h/d, dynamic 1m-1h; recurring tasks expire 7 days after creation; session-scoped, max 50/session; .claude/loop.md replaces only the bare-/loop maintenance prompt; restored on --resume if unexpired.
claims.verifier_evidence: Loops die with the session
claims.gsr_application: In-day babysitting only; standing schedules go to Routines or Supabase Cron

claims.claim: Routines (research preview): Anthropic cloud, fresh GitHub clone, NO permission prompts, push only to claude/-prefixed branches; triggers scheduled (CLI /schedule) / API /fire / GitHub events (web-only); caps Pro 5 / Max 15 / Team 25 runs/day, min interval 1h.
claims.verifier_evidence: Structurally cannot touch QNAP/ProPresenter (clone + connectors only)
claims.gsr_application: Nightly data audits, RC dry-run reports, lanes refresh: the laptop-closed layer (14A)

claims.claim: Agent teams (CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS): still experimental + disabled by default as of 2026-06-11; no /resume of teammates, one team at a time, much higher token cost.
claims.verifier_evidence: Requires v2.1.32+
claims.gsr_application: Stay on subagents/background sessions; at most a one-off adversarial review

claims.claim: Hooks now span ~30 events and FIVE handler types: command, http, mcp_tool, prompt, agent (experimental). SessionStart/Setup: command+mcp_tool only.
claims.verifier_evidence: Tutorial at /hooks-guide
claims.gsr_application: Mechanical rule enforcement: PreToolUse command hook hard-blocking Tailscale-IP/QNAP-write commands; prompt Stop hook as automated David-Rule check

claims.claim: Agent SDK: list_sessions()/listSessions() real and returns auto-generated per-session summary (SDKSessionInfo.summary); transcript reading is get_session_messages() NOT 'Session.History'; official cookbook 'Building a session browser' exists.
claims.verifier_evidence: Mission doc's 'Session.History' is wrong - correction recorded
claims.gsr_application: Mission Control extend-lanes build: ~20-line script list_sessions() -> lanes.json sessions block -> lanes.html

claims.claim: Session storage: ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<session-id>.jsonl, 30-day default cleanup, --continue/--resume <name|id>, --from-pr <number>, named sessions (claude -n, /rename), /branch + --fork-session.
claims.verifier_evidence: Verbatim doc quote in agent report
claims.gsr_application: Name lane sessions so claude --resume lower-thirds works; raise cleanupPeriodDays (CL-008)

claims.claim: /effort: low/medium/high/xhigh/max on Fable 5 + Opus 4.8/4.7; default high (xhigh on 4.7); ultracode = setting (xhigh + dynamic workflows), session-only; 'ultrathink' the only special keyword now.
claims.gsr_application: Lanes default high; xhigh/ultracode only for hard builds; maps to Weight/Fragility/API-cost scoring

claims.claim: Checkpointing: /rewind or Esc Esc (only with empty prompt); bash-modified files NOT tracked, nor external edits/other sessions; rewind menu has Summarize from/up-to here.
claims.gsr_application: Git discipline + Type-YES gates stay the safety net for scripted ops

claims.claim: Changelog May 1 - Jun 11: /goal (2.1.139), Agent View (2.1.139-145), auto mode expanded (2.1.143-152), background sessions in /resume + pinned stay alive (2.1.144/147), dynamic workflows + Opus 4.8 xhigh (2.1.154), Stop/SubagentStop hooks return additionalContext (2.1.163), fallbackModel chains of 3 (2.1.166), post-session hook + /cd + --safe-mode (2.1.169), Fable 5 (2.1.170), nested subagents 5 deep (2.1.172).
claims.verifier_evidence: Version-date mapping via code.claude.com/docs/en/whats-new
claims.gsr_application: The 5-10 lanes can run as supervised background sessions that verify their own completion; fallbackModel for unattended resilience

claims.claim: Agent View shows NO token counts (spend lives in bare /goal status); background via /bg, /background, left-arrow, claude --bg (not '/agent'); Ctrl+B backgrounds running bash/agents.
claims.verifier_evidence: Mission doc table slightly off; corrected in run-notes
claims.gsr_application: Pair Agent View with ccusage as the cost lens (CL-013)

claims.claim: ergut/youtube-transcript-mcp (remote, Cloudflare Workers): live and responding 2026-06-11; transcript fetch executes on Cloudflare's edge IP, not the session's; 7-day KV cache; no keys.
claims.verifier_evidence: claude mcp add --transport sse youtube-transcript https://youtube-transcript-mcp.ergut.workers.dev/sse; thin maintenance (1-person)
claims.gsr_application: The ONLY vetted path that may unblock transcript mining from CLOUD sessions; INSTALL candidate (one-tap yes, then live-test whether YouTube serves Cloudflare)

claims.claim: jkawamoto/mcp-youtube-transcript (local, uvx, yt-dlp backend): best-maintained local transcript MCP; auto-paginates transcripts >50k chars (fits 58-min episodes).
claims.verifier_evidence: v0.5.7 Jan 2026, deps bumped Feb 2026, CI green; runs on the session's own IP (Mac only)
claims.gsr_application: INSTALL candidate for Daniel's Mac (one-tap yes); pairs with ergut remote for full coverage; @sinco-lab variant SKIPped (weaker)

claims.claim: claude-self-reflect install-safety: PASSES the bar - default fully local (SQLite + FastEmbed in one binary), no keys, no Docker; only AI-Narratives feature is opt-in cloud; v8.2.0 notes show active security fixes.
claims.verifier_evidence: Caveat: indexes everything incl. accidentally-pasted secrets (locally); installs 6 hooks; pin version + skim setup script
claims.gsr_application: Safe to propose; final adopt call deferred to Mission Control phase (CL-012 WATCH stands)

claims.claim: supabase/agent-skills (FIRST-PARTY): two instruction-only skills (supabase, postgres-best-practices); pure SKILL.md, no code execution; the Supabase MCP itself recommends it.
claims.verifier_evidence: v0.1.5 2026-05-27, 2.2k stars; npx skills add supabase/agent-skills
claims.gsr_application: INSTALL candidate (near-zero risk): accuracy boost on every migration/RLS/edge-function task

claims.claim: vercel-labs/next-skills (FIRST-PARTY): instruction-only skills explicitly covering Next.js 16 (cacheComponents, use cache, cacheLife/cacheTag/updateTag) - the exact training-data gap the repo warns about.
claims.verifier_evidence: Active 2026; note Vercel's own evals found AGENTS.md slightly outperforms skills - complements our existing apps/dashboard/AGENTS.md
claims.gsr_application: INSTALL candidate (near-zero risk): npx skills add vercel-labs/next-skills --skill next-best-practices

claims.claim: commit-commands plugin (official Anthropic marketplace): one-command commit/push/PR flows; first-party.
claims.verifier_evidence: /plugin install commit-commands@claude-plugins-official; PR-based flow matches repo branch rules
claims.gsr_application: INSTALL candidate: cuts the most frequent manual chore; sibling pr-review-toolkit SKIPped (duplicates built-in review skills)

claims.claim: georgeantonopoulos/Basecamp-MCP-Server: 75 tools over Basecamp API incl. writes; BUT stores OAuth tokens in plaintext oauth_tokens.json by default (violates the 1Password/env-only bar) unless redirected via BASECAMP_MCP_TOKEN_FILE.
claims.verifier_evidence: 91 commits, no tagged releases, 89 stars; third-party write access needs a code skim
claims.gsr_application: WATCH: highest input-reduction potential but needs a 30-min vetting pass (token storage + write-path review) before proposing to Daniel; Lane 5 builds its own refresh loop regardless (CL-021)

claims.claim: alxpark/propresenter-mcp: full ProPresenter REST API as MCP (231 endpoints incl. write/trigger ops); local-network to port 50000.
claims.gsr_application: WATCH: only ever pointable at the TEST machine; live rig keeps the in-the-moment human yes (canon s15); candidate for the ProPresenter lane's test-machine phase

claims.claim: Whisper transcription skills exist (jftuga/transcript-critic, madappgang transcription skill) but Daniel's Mac already runs a local whisper pipeline at ~/Productions.
claims.gsr_application: SKIP unless the existing pipeline breaks

claims.claim: Vibe Kanban lost its company (Bloop shut down April 2026), now community-maintained - removes it as a Mission Control candidate.
claims.verifier_evidence: Single secondary source; not needed anyway given Agent View decision
claims.gsr_application: Confirms SKIP of third-party orchestrators

claims.claim: PR #50 ships the rename migration + ~20 call-site updates + display_duration/last_line on production_graphics; PR #52 (stacked) DEFERS the Title->Intro Graphic CHECK+data standardization to a follow-up migration, sets the validator to soft amber >65 (66-70 allowed, never blocks) with extraction prompt 60-65/70-ceiling, and persists Var 1/Var 2 into lower_thirds_variations slots 2/3 (slot 1 = primary) across all three write paths.
claims.verifier_evidence: PR bodies read via GitHub MCP 2026-06-11; #52 explains the deferral (constraint-before-cleanup aborted the migration)
claims.gsr_application: Slice 1 scope: 1.1 (Intro Graphic follow-up migration) still needed; 1.2 becomes a reconcile (add under-55 amber + over-70 hard block per canon s13 if missing); 1.5 shrinks to post-merge verify + the 3 topic-relevant chyron variations

claims.claim: WhisperKit v1.0.0 (May 2026, repo renamed argmax-oss-swift) bundles SpeakerKit for speaker labels; its 2.2% WER is a vendor benchmark.
claims.verifier_evidence: Repo-verified fact sheet dated 2026-06-04; re-verify currency at 7.2 build time per never-stale rule
claims.gsr_application: Transcription build (7.2) stays on the canon registry pick: WhisperKit + SpeakerKit local, WhisperX honorable fallback
